data.text: Bazarr+ v2.1.0 (LiveWire) is out! Ships a full subtitle editor inside Bazarr+: video preview with audio track selection, waveform timeline, inline cue editing, ffsubsync auto-sync, AI translation per cue, and 30+ keyboard shortcuts. Plus supervisor-backed startup with live SSE progress, request-a-stop on running jobs, opt-in subdl anime/pack support, and three upstream sync fixes that prevent silent stall conditions in long-running instances. Landing site: lavx.github.io/bazarr.

New sister project: Arrstack. The arr stack, pre-wired. One command installs qBittorrent, Prowlarr, Sonarr, Radarr, Bazarr+, Jellyfin, and Jellyseerr on a clean Linux host in about 90 seconds, with API key cross-wiring, indexer tagging, and service bootstrapping handled automatically. No YAML, no wizard tours. Try it at lavx.github.io/arrstack.

Bazarr+ v2.0.0 (Eclipse) is here! We've officially become a hard fork with independent development and versioning. This release brings provider priority search, OpenSubtitles.org scraper, AI translation via 300+ LLMs, 11 batch operations, subtitle viewer, advanced filters, encrypted API keys, and a complete theme redesign. All tracking has been removed. Thank you for being part of this. Note: config changes are not backwards-compatible with upstream Bazarr, so keep a backup if you ever need to switch back.

The subtitle scoring system has been updated from upstream. If you notice subtitles not being downloaded that previously worked, lower your Minimum Score in Settings > Subtitles. The new default is 80% (previously 90%) because release-specific attributes now carry more weight in the score calculation.
